18.reason: [The input may not end with exponent 0; the problem states only non-zero terms are given, and the polynomial may not have a constant term (x^0). Thus, waiting for n==0 to stop reading is incorrect and may cause infinite loop or buffer overflow.]

66.reason: [If the result polynomial is empty (e.g., multiplication yields zero polynomial), head->next is NULL, so dereferencing temp causes undefined behavior. Also, do-while runs at least once even when no terms exist.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [If the result polynomial is empty (e.g., multiplication yields zero polynomial), head->next is NULL, so dereferencing temp causes undefined behavior. Also, do-while runs at least once even when no terms exist.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [If result polynomial is zero (all coefficients cancel), head->next is NULL, causing dereference of NULL pointer.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [If result polynomial is zero (all coefficients cancel), head->next is NULL, leading to dereferencing NULL pointer in first printf.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [If the result polynomial is zero (all coefficients cancel out), head->next may be NULL, causing dereference of NULL pointer and undefined behavior. Also, using 'do-while' assumes at least one term exists.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [If the result polynomial is empty (e.g., multiplication yields zero polynomial), head->next is NULL, so temp is NULL, and this loop will attempt to dereference NULL in the first iteration, causing runtime crash. Also, the problem says only non-zero terms should be output, but zero coefficients from combination are not removed.]

81.reason: [If head->next is NULL (empty list), q is NULL, and accessing q->n in the loop condition causes segmentation fault. Also, the loop condition checks q!=NULL after using q, leading to null pointer dereference.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [If head->next is NULL (empty list), q is NULL, and accessing q->n in the loop condition causes segmentation fault. Also, the loop condition checks q!=NULL after using q, leading to null pointer dereference.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [If head->next is NULL (empty list), q is NULL, and accessing q->n causes segmentation fault. Also, loop condition should be while(q != NULL) before accessing members.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Function may reach end without returning a value if head->next is NULL (empty list), causing undefined behavior. Also, loop condition should be while(q != NULL) but logic inside assumes q is valid.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [If head->next is NULL (empty list), q is NULL, and accessing q->n causes segmentation fault. Also, loop condition should be checked before body to avoid NULL dereference.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [If head->next is NULL (empty list), q is NULL, and the do-while loop will still execute once, causing dereference of NULL pointer. Also, function may not return anything in all paths if list is empty or logic fails, leading to undefined behavior.]
